title: Seselinia serbica
taxonomy: Seselinia serbica is classified within the family Apiaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This family consists of a diverse range of species, many of which are used as food crops or in the production of spices and herbs. Seselinia serbica is further classified within the genus Seseli, which is a group of flowering plants native to the Mediterranean region. Overall, Seselinia serbica's classification reflects its unique characteristics and its place within a larger group of flowering plants.

intro: Seselinia serbica, also known as mountain savory, is a perennial plant species within the genus Seselinia, belonging to the family Apiaceae. This plant has been found to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of respiratory issues and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Seselinia serbica include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Seselinia serbica is often used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to alleviate coughs, colds, and other respiratory ailments.
